trigger_pattern: Unlimited liability or uncapped liability language
reason: Unlimited liability exposes the company to disproportionate financial risk relative to contract value
recommended_action: Propose liability cap at 12 months of fees paid; escalate to General Counsel if buyer insists

trigger_pattern: Uptime commitment above 99.9% or custom SLA terms
reason: Our infrastructure is architected for 99.9% availability; commitments above this require additional investment and carry penalty risk
recommended_action: Offer 99.9% standard SLA; any higher commitment requires CTO and Finance approval

trigger_pattern: Service credits exceeding 3% of monthly fees per incident
reason: Excessive service credits can significantly impact deal profitability and set unfavorable precedent
recommended_action: Propose standard service credit schedule (1% per 0.1% below SLA); escalate exceptions to Finance

trigger_pattern: Specific country or region data residency requirement beyond US and EU
reason: Custom data residency may require new infrastructure deployment and ongoing operational costs
recommended_action: Confirm Azure region availability; estimate incremental cost; require minimum 3-year commitment for custom residency

trigger_pattern: Commitment to deliver specific features by a specific date
reason: Roadmap commitments create contractual obligations that constrain product development flexibility and carry penalty risk
recommended_action: Never commit to specific features or dates in contracts; offer roadmap briefings and advisory board participation instead

trigger_pattern: Buyer requests right to conduct their own security audit or penetration test
reason: Direct security audits by buyers create operational overhead and potential exposure of security architecture details
recommended_action: Offer SOC 2 report and annual pentest summary; if insufficient offer supervised audit with 30-day advance notice

reason: Source code escrow introduces IP management complexity and ongoing administrative burden
recommended_action: Agree to escrow with standard release conditions (bankruptcy; cessation of business; material breach not cured in 90 days)

reason: MFN clauses constrain future pricing flexibility and can trigger retroactive adjustments across the customer base
recommended_action: Decline MFN clauses; offer competitive pricing commitment for the specific deal instead

reason: Uncapped indemnity creates unlimited financial exposure particularly for IP and data breach claims
recommended_action: Propose indemnity cap equal to limitation of liability; carve-outs for IP infringement only with mutual cap

trigger_pattern: Penalties or liquidated damages for missed implementation milestones
reason: Implementation timelines depend on buyer cooperation; penalties for delays create one-sided risk exposure
recommended_action: Include mutual dependency language; propose timeline extensions for buyer-caused delays; cap penalties at 5% of implementation fees

reason: CJIS compliance requires specific technical controls | background checks | and ongoing audit obligations that we currently meet but must maintain
recommended_action: Confirm current CJIS compliance status; ensure contract includes mutual obligations for maintaining compliance

reason: FedRAMP authorization is a multi-year process requiring significant investment; we currently hold StateRAMP but not FedRAMP
recommended_action: Clarify whether StateRAMP is acceptable; if FedRAMP is mandatory this may be a no-bid unless timeline allows 18+ months

trigger_pattern: Insurance requirements exceeding $5M per occurrence or $10M aggregate
reason: Increasing insurance coverage above current policy limits requires additional premium costs that impact deal economics
recommended_action: Confirm current coverage levels; obtain quote for incremental coverage; include cost in deal pricing

trigger_pattern: Requirement to accept buyer paper (buyer's contract template) without negotiation
reason: Buyer paper often contains unfavorable terms that may not be identified without thorough legal review
recommended_action: Request adequate time for legal review (minimum 2 weeks); identify key terms requiring modification; escalate non-negotiable buyer terms

trigger_pattern: Requirement that data never leave a specific jurisdiction even for disaster recovery
reason: Strict single-jurisdiction requirements may prevent effective disaster recovery and limit our operational flexibility
recommended_action: Assess feasibility of single-region DR; propose within-jurisdiction backup solution; price incremental infrastructure cost
